about: I'm a developer working at the intersection of artificial intelligence and data engineering. I build systems that make sense of large, messy datasets — from pipelines to interfaces. Currently focused on applied ML, LLM tooling, and developer experience. I care about legible code, fast feedback loops, and tools that get out of the way.

bullets: Built and maintained data pipelines processing 10M+ records/day using Python and Apache Kafka.
Designed a feature store abstraction that reduced ML model deployment time by 40%.
Led migration of legacy ETL workflows to a modern orchestration layer (Prefect).
Collaborated closely with ML engineers to productionise model serving infrastructure.
Developed dashboards and reporting pipelines using dbt and BigQuery.
Automated data quality checks, reducing manual QA overhead by 60%.
Collaborated with product teams to define metrics and KPI frameworks.

projects.title: LLM Eval Harness
projects.description: An open-source evaluation framework for comparing LLM outputs across prompts, models, and datasets. Supports custom metrics, side-by-side diffs, and exports to JSONL for offline analysis.

projects.title: DataLineage CLI
projects.description: A command-line tool that parses SQL and dbt models to generate interactive lineage graphs, helping teams understand data dependencies at a glance.

projects.title: Semantic Search Engine
projects.description: A lightweight semantic search system built on sentence-transformers and FAISS, with a clean React frontend for querying local document collections.

items.label: How I think about data quality
Why observability matters in ML pipelines
Agentic AI systems and multi-step reasoning
